contract.goal: Wire the first jelly verb: jelly research <slug-or-topic> --research-file=<path> --notes-file=<path> [--no-commit] files RESEARCH.md + RESEARCH-NOTES.md into projects/<slug>/ and commits. Simpler than loom's research — NO events.jsonl, NO manifest, NO config at research time (the manifest comes from jelly plan in U4; jelly has no event log). Brings in the shared git + slug helpers the remaining verbs reuse.
acceptance_criteria: cli/lib/types.ts — CliContext (projectsRoot, repoRoot?, today?, cwdOverride?, gitRunner?) + DispatchResult, moved out of jelly.ts so verbs + entry share them without an import cycle. jelly.ts imports from here.
cli/lib/git.ts — GitRunner type + defaultGitRunner (isCommitted, addAndCommit), JellyError-based; injectable for tests.
cli/lib/project.ts — createSlug(topic, today) + DATE_RE + SLUG_RE; JellyError-based.

cli/verbs/research.ts — researchVerb + RESEARCH_VERBS. Resolves slug, validates source files exist, creates projects/<slug>/, copies the two files, collision-checks RESEARCH.md (committed→refuse research-exists-committed; uncommitted→overwrite), commits [jelly research] <slug> unless --no-commit, emits JSON. NO manifest/config/events writes.
jelly.ts wires RESEARCH_VERBS into VERBS_BY_NAMESPACE (research stops being not-implemented).
jelly.test.ts updated: the not-implemented test.each now covers only the still-unwired namespaces (plan/revise/adr); a new test proves research routes to the verb (no topic → missing-args).
cli/verbs/research.test.ts — verb behavior with an injected gitRunner; npm test passes.
rules_applied: PLAN.md § Phase 1.3 (jelly research verb) + § Decisions (no events log; manifest written by plan, not research).
plugins/loom/cli/verbs/loom/research.ts + lib/git.ts + lib/project.ts as structural references (minus loom-adopt/events/config).
Panel: dropped the auto-derived evaluator-react-api (documented mis-derivation for Node .ts — memory derive-panel-react-api-misderivation). Ran contract-fit + test-unit + naming.
disqualifiers: Writing a manifest, config, or events file (jelly research files only the 2 dossier files; the manifest is U4's jelly plan).
A research skill (jelly has CLI verbs; content authoring is the agent/operator's job upstream).

actions: Wrote cli/lib/types.ts: CliContext (projectsRoot + repoRoot + today + cwdOverride + gitRunner) + DispatchResult, extracted from jelly.ts so the entry + verbs share them without an import cycle. jelly.ts now imports + re-exports them (the re-export keeps jelly.test.ts's `import type { CliContext } from './jelly.ts'` working).

Wrote cli/lib/git.ts: GitRunner type + defaultGitRunner (isCommitted via git ls-files --error-unmatch; addAndCommit via git add + git commit), JellyError('git-commit-failed'). Mirrors loom's git.ts; injectable via CliContext.gitRunner.
Wrote cli/lib/project.ts: createSlug (date-prefix + kebab the topic, min 2 chars) + DATE_RE + SLUG_RE. JellyError('invalid-date' / 'invalid-topic'). Mirrors loom's slug logic.

Wrote cli/verbs/research.ts: researchVerb. parseArgs(research-file/notes-file/no-commit/pretty); requires the positional + both file flags (missing-args); existsSync source-file checks (research-file-not-found / notes-file-not-found); SLUG_RE picks slug-verbatim vs createSlug-from-topic; mkdir projects/<slug>/ + copyFileSync the two files; collision check via gitRunner.isCommitted (committed→research-exists-committed, uncommitted→overwrite for failed-commit recovery); addAndCommit [jelly research] <slug> unless --no-commit; emits {slug, path, research_path, notes_path, committed}. No manifest/config/events code path. Exports RESEARCH_VERBS.

Wired jelly.ts: imported types from lib/types.ts + re-exported; imported RESEARCH_VERBS; VERBS_BY_NAMESPACE = { research: RESEARCH_VERBS } (research now routes to the verb instead of not-implemented).

Updated jelly.test.ts: UNWIRED_NAMESPACES = [plan, revise, adr] (the not-implemented sweep); a research-routes-to-verb test (no topic → missing-args, proving the verbless routing wires research); a no-gaps tripwire ([...UNWIRED, 'research'].sort() === Object.keys(NAMESPACES).sort()) so adding/removing a namespace forces the bookkeeping update.

Wrote cli/verbs/research.test.ts (14 tests): a recording fakeGit (controllable isCommitted, records commits) + beforeEach/afterEach mkdtempSync isolation. Covers happy path (topic→slug, files copied, committed, message asserted), slug-verbatim, the no-manifest/config/events discipline (4 files asserted absent + only 2 staged paths), --no-commit, missing-args ×3, source-not-found ×2, collision (committed→refuse + uncommitted→overwrite-replaces-content).

Verified: npm test → 1045 passed across 71 files (was 1033; +12 net). E2E smoke via the bin (JELLY_PROJECTS_ROOT=<tmp> jelly research demo-topic ... --no-commit) created exactly RESEARCH.md + RESEARCH-NOTES.md, no manifest/config/events. Panel (contract-fit + test-unit + naming): all three approved on first run. Dropped evaluator-react-api (documented Node-.ts mis-derivation).

changes_since_previous: Third unit of Phase 1.3; the first jelly VERB (U1 was the shell, U2 the manifest lib). Composes on U1's dispatch + U2's error class. Exercises the verbless-namespace routing for the first time (the U1 panel flagged it as untested-until-a-verb-lands; this unit lands it + tests it). Brings in the shared git + slug + types libs that U4 (plan), U5 (revise), U6 (adr) all reuse. Carries lagged pr-opened event for PR #60 in events.jsonl.
notes_for_pr: First jelly verb. The jelly/loom divergence is the headline: loom's research auto-adopts the loom substrate (writes manifest.json + config.json + events.jsonl) and emits research-started/completed events; jelly's research deliberately writes ONLY the two dossier files. jelly's manifest is write-once at plan time (U4), and jelly keeps no event log (PLAN.md § Decisions 'No events log'). The no-manifest/config/events discipline is pinned by an explicit test (asserts those 4 files absent + only 2 paths staged) and confirmed by the e2e bin smoke.

Shared libs introduced (reused by U4-U6): lib/types.ts (CliContext/DispatchResult, extracted so the entry + verbs avoid an import cycle), lib/git.ts (GitRunner — the testability seam; tests inject a fake, production shells to git), lib/project.ts (createSlug + slug regexes). All mirror loom's equivalents, JellyError-based.

Verbless-routing now exercised: the U1 panel flagged the VERBLESS_NAMESPACES routing branch as unreachable-until-a-verb-lands. This unit lands the first verb through it; the research-routes-to-verb test (no topic → missing-args, NOT not-implemented) proves the routing works. A no-gaps tripwire test keeps the UNWIRED_NAMESPACES bookkeeping in sync with the registry.

Advisory from contract-fit (non-gating): the emitted JSON mixes `path` (absolute targetDir) with `research_path`/`notes_path` (bare relative filenames). This matches loom's research output shape (loom emits absolute `path` + relative research_path/notes_path too), so it's a deliberate mirror, but a consumer should know the asymmetry. Left as-is for loom parity.

Test-hygiene note (from test-unit, explicitly NOT flagged): research.test.ts uses beforeEach/afterEach (exception-safe cleanup — the U1 advisory's recommended pattern); jelly.test.ts retains end-of-body rmSync (mirrors loom.test.ts, unchanged this unit). Mixing is acceptable — new code sets the better precedent; rewriting jelly.test.ts would smuggle an unrelated cleanup into this diff. jelly.test.ts migrates when independently touched.
Substrate-state carry-over: lagged pr-opened event for PR #60 in events.jsonl.

U4 (jelly plan) is next — the heavy verb. It's the first consumer of U2's manifest lib (writes manifest.toml), writes PLAN.md + INTERVIEW.md, instantiates the jelly-guild CLAUDE.md template (Phase 1.2 U5), and manages the @projects/<slug>/CLAUDE.md import line in the repo-root CLAUDE.md (the Phase 1.1 mechanism). It will need repoRoot from CliContext + the jelly-guild template path.
